name: workplace_health_cold_chain_excursion_oakline_auto_ben
premise: Stakeholders disagree on what counts as verified fact versus prudent assumption. A cross-functional incident desk is managing a domain incident with incomplete information and high reputational exposure. The situation maps to internal runbooks and external messaging tied to: workplace health cold chain excursion oakline auto ben. The moral tension is explicitly framed as autonomy versus beneficence: leadership cannot fully satisfy both without accepting tradeoffs. Two upstream teams have published contradictory status pages; customers are comparing timestamps. The breach-notification and customer-communication clock has started; executive staff expect a single coherent position before the next press cycle. Any exception must be time-boxed, named, and reversible where technically feasible. Preserve chain-of-custody for evidence; no bulk deletion or retention-policy changes during the incident.

agents.name: Mei Sato
agents.identity: I am Mei Sato, the accountable decision-maker on this Workplace Health bridge; I own what gets logged, communicated, and executed in the next window.
agents.goal: I need to stop the bleeding without baking in a rule that later punishes beneficence whenever someone invokes autonomy.

agents.name: Hector Morales
agents.identity: I am Hector Morales, responsible for surfacing who gets protected last when systems fail; I am watching for silent harm and unfair distribution.
agents.goal: I want safeguards so beneficence is not sacrificed by default just because autonomy feels morally loud in the moment.

choices: I will choose a narrow, time-boxed authorization with explicit human override, subgroup monitoring, and a hard stop if harm indicators rise—accepting slower relief to protect beneficence.

I will choose a broad immediate authorization to stabilize outcomes fast, with transparent metrics and rapid correction afterward—accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: External parties will infer a decision if none is posted. With partial evidence, Mei Sato must decide under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Mei Sato do?
